currentSession.title: Mastering Risk Management in Volatile Forex Markets

presenter.name: Dr. Omar Al-Farsi
presenter.bio: 15+ years FX experience, CISI qualified, former HSBC trading desk. Regular speaker at Money Expo, iFX EXPO, and Forex Expo Dubai.

slides.title: Why Risk Management Matters
bullets: 90% of retail traders lose money — risk management is the differentiator
Volatility is not the enemy — unmanaged exposure is
Case study: CHF flash crash 2015 — lessons learned

slides.title: The 2% Rule & Position Sizing
bullets: Never risk more than 2% of account equity per trade
Adapting position size to volatility (ATR-based sizing)

slides.title: Stop Loss Strategies
bullets: Fixed pip stops vs. volatility-based stops
Trailing stops: when and how to use them
The hidden danger of mental stops — always use hard stops

slides.title: Correlation & Portfolio Risk
bullets: Trading EUR/USD and GBP/USD = double exposure to USD
Correlation matrix: identifying hidden portfolio risk

slides.title: Risk Management Tools
bullets: MT5 built-in risk tools and margin calculator
Third-party tools: Myfxbook, FX Blue, Trade Analytics
Automated risk management with Expert Advisors

slides.title: Q&A — Ask Me Anything
bullets: Submit your questions via the Q&A panel
Top questions will be answered live
Book a 1-on-1 session for personalised advice

seedQuestions.question: What's the optimal risk-reward ratio for day trading forex?
seedQuestions.answer: I recommend a minimum of 1:2 risk-reward for day trading. This means if your stop loss is 20 pips, your take profit should be at least 40 pips. This way, even with a 40% win rate, you remain profitable.

seedQuestions.question: How do you handle risk during major news events like NFP?
seedQuestions.answer: During high-impact news, I recommend either closing positions 30 minutes before the release, or reducing position size by 50%. Alternatively, use options strategies to hedge. Never increase leverage before news events.

seedQuestions.question: Is it better to use a fixed lot size or scale based on account equity?
seedQuestions.answer: Always scale based on equity — this is what the 2% rule is about. Fixed lot sizes don't adapt to your growing or shrinking account. Use the formula: Position Size = (Equity × 0.02) / (SL in pips × pip value).
